rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee contracts in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for bindings like assign only when the left-hand side is a visible output or declared auxiliary variable.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts that relate visible inputs to visible outputs.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must reside within valid AADL component type or implementation scopes, with proper visibility of referenced identifiers.
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths or ports.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings such as assignments must reference valid outputs or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees or subcomponent path references in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not found in the input AADL model.
